channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Dominion voting systems is still a GOP bogeyman ahead of the election
item.description: The election is nearly here, and Dominion Voting Systems is once again on Republicans’ minds. The voting machine-maker became the subject of conspiracy theories following the 2020 election, which Donald Trump falsely claims was rigged against him.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Gluware expands network automation platform with AI copilots, GitHub integration
item.description: Gluware today announced a series of upcoming features for its network automation platform, designed to streamline network management and reduce downtime across diverse network environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Alabama man arrested for hacking Securities and Exchange Commission X account
item.description: Following the unauthorized announcement made during the hack, the DOJ said the price of bitcoin increased by more than $1,000 per bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Logan Sparkman, NewsNation

item.title: Global cybersecurity talent gap widens
item.description: Despite some recent positive news for tech hiring, a growing cybersecurity talent shortage threatens to hamstring businesses. Data from CyberSeek shows that in the U.S., some 265,000 more cybersecurity workers are needed to solve current staffing needs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI 네이티브 소프트웨어 엔지니어링 시대, 생각보다 빨리 올 수도
item.description: 가트너의 소프트웨어 엔지니어링 부문 수석 책임 애널리스트인 필립 월시는 많은 개발 작업을 완전히 자동화하거나 부분적으로 도울 수 있는 AI 코딩 에이전트가 등장하고 있다며, 2025년에는 더 많은 파일럿 프로그램과 개념 증명이 출시될 것으로 예상된다고 말했다.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 11 key roles for AI success
item.description: More companies in every industry are adopting AI to transform business processes. But the success of their initiatives depends on having the right people on board, not just data and technology.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Here’s how Cleary Gottlieb law firm uses genAI for pre-trial discovery and more
item.description: Corporate law is nothing like you see on television. To prepare for a case, 150 attorneys might be tasked to travel to remote warehouses to comb through tens of millions of documents gathering dust or track down amorphous electronic communications. It’s a process known as discovery.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 10 Insider Previews: A guide to the builds
item.description: Microsoft never sleeps. In addition to its steady releases of major and minor updates to the current version of Windows 10, the company frequently rolls out public preview builds to members of its Windows Insider Program, allowing them to test out — and even help shape — upcoming features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why you shouldn’t store your money in payment apps
item.description: As use of payment apps has grown in recent years, the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau has issued guidance on best practices to avoid pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI-powered scam targets Gmail users
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI-powered scam targets 2.5 billion Gmail users in sophisticated phishing attacks
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about a new AI-powered scam that targets Gmail users and is harder to detect unless you're very cautious.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google endorses California GOP Rep. Issa’s bill to reveal funders backing lawsuits
item.description: The bill, introduced last week would require plaintiffs to reveal any investors who could make money based on the outcome of a case. It would also require parties to disclose the financing agreement between investors and those involved.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Russia, China collaborating with criminal networks in cyberattacks against adversaries: Microsoft
item.description: Russia, China and Iran are increasingly collaborating with cybercrime networks to launch a variety of attacks against their adversaries, including the U.S., Microsoft said in a Tuesday report.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How Ernst & Young’s AI platform is ‘radically’ reshaping operations
item.description: Multinational consultancy Ernst & Young (EY) said generative AI (genAI) is “radically reshaping” the way it operates, and the company boasts a 96% adoption rate of the technology by employees. After spending $1.4 billion on a customized generative AI platform called EY.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: HUD aims to tackle AI skills gap with new assessment approach
item.description: A new skills benchmark model at HUD will assess where employees are in their understanding of AI and recommend a personalized path for training and development. The post HUD aims to tackle AI skills gap with new assessment approach first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Ktor 3.0 switches to kotlinx.io library
item.description: Ktor 3.0, the latest version of the Kotlin-based framework for building asynchronous client- and server-side applications, is now available. Ktor 3.0 switches to the kotlinx.io library for handling data sources. The release also highlights support for server-sent events (SSE).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoT driving IT modernization to meet enterprise, local needs
item.description: Cordell Schachter, the Transportation Department CIO, said the agency’s DevSecOps capabilities are maturing as part of its digital transformation effort. The post DoT driving IT modernization to meet enterprise, local needs first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: MoneyGram hack exposes customers' sensitive data, cripples services
item.description: MoneyGram, an American payment and money transfer platform, confirmed a data breach that exposed sensitive customer data to hackers, forcing its services offline for five days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biometric data: Is it safe to hand it over to any company that asks?
item.description: Biometric data, like face and fingerprint scans, can help you save time when accessing your personal data – but with it comes a whole host of privacy risks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
